core_principles: Content is King: The UI must completely dissolve when the user is reading.
Tactile Utility: Inputs and buttons should feel physical, using 1px borders and sharp/slight radii instead of soft shadows.
Typographic Tension: Contrast sterile, functional sans-serif UI elements with warm, elegant serif reading content.
Eye Comfort: Never use pure #FFFFFF for reading backgrounds; use a warm paper tint in light mode and a deep matte charcoal in dark mode.

typography.rules: DO NOT import custom fonts to keep the bundle small and native-feeling. Use Android's default system fonts.

home_screen.layout: Centered vertical stack with generous negative space.

elements.name: App Title
elements.style: h1_title, heavily tracked, top-aligned with significant top margin.

elements.name: URL Input
elements.style: Large tap target (min-height 56px), 1px solid border, subtle background tint. MUST include a 'Paste' quick-action icon inside or near the input.

elements.name: Read Button
elements.style: Full-width, stark contrast (Black in light mode, White in dark mode), sharp or slightly rounded corners (4px max), bold sans-serif text.

elements.name: Theme Toggle
elements.style: Minimalist icon-only button placed in the top-right corner or bottom center.

loading_state.visuals: Do not use a generic spinner. Use a typographic pulse effect on the word 'Resolving...' or 'Fetching snapshot...', or a minimalist indeterminate horizontal progress bar (1px height).

top_bar.layout: Minimalist. Flex row. Back button on left, source domain name (e.g., 'nytimes.com') in center (caption style, uppercase, widely tracked), Theme toggle on right.
top_bar.style: Solid background matching theme. 1px bottom border to separate from content.

content_area.typography: Article title uses h2_article_title. Body uses body_reader (18sp, 1.6 line height).
content_area.images: Scaled to screen width minus padding, subtle 2px border radius, caption text underneath.

error_state.visuals: Red accent color for an icon (e.g., an 'X' or broken link), surrounded by high-contrast sans-serif text explaining the error.

input.base: min-height 56px, padding-horizontal 16, fontSize 16, border-1 border-border, bg-surface, color-text_primary, border-radius-4

motion_and_interactions.screen_transitions: Standard Android horizontal slide or fade. Do not over-animate.
motion_and_interactions.theme_switch: Smooth crossfade if possible, otherwise instant.

accessibility_and_testing.test_ids: Every interactive element must have a data-testid mapped (or testID for React Native) matching kebab-case descriptors.
accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure text contrast against background exceeds 4.5:1. The chosen palette guarantees this.

media.notes: No external images required for UI. Content images are parsed dynamically from the article HTML.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use React Native StyleSheet for styling. Do not assume web CSS paradigms like hover states; focus on touch targets and activeOpacity.
Ensure the URL parser and routing logic handles intents from the Android Share Sheet.
Implement the reader view using a native ScrollView or a robust HTML renderer (like react-native-render-html) mapped to the typographic scales provided.
Respect the 'No custom fonts' rule. Stick to native 'sans-serif' and 'serif' for optimal performance and native feel.
